code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 2 Protocol Design, no bioinformatics code component

reliability.note: Out-of-scope redirect present but no missing-input structured fallback for partially-specified inputs

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 350 lines; references directory offloads depth but main file is near upper bound

human_usability.note: Description is accurate but does not use highly natural trigger phrasing that non-expert users would readily produce

maintainability.note: Seven reference modules well separated; testability slightly limited by lack of example test inputs in SKILL.md

agent_specific.note: Progressive disclosure is good but SKILL.md length approaching 500-line threshold; composability has minor friction due to full-output mandate

inputs.label: Prognostic biomarker study in heart failure with BNP/EF/mortality

assertions.text: Output includes a clearly labeled primary aim that is singular and answerable
At least one formal hypothesis is written in falsifiable, directional form
Confirmatory and exploratory components are explicitly separated
No references, PMIDs, or validation data are fabricated
Output stays within protocol-framing scope and does not produce a full methods plan

inputs.label: Single-cell therapy resistance project in NSCLC

assertions.text: Output includes a clearly labeled primary aim that is singular and answerable
Hypothesis is appropriately marked as exploratory/hypothesis-generating where mechanistic evidence is insufficient
Confirmatory and exploratory components are explicitly separated
No fabricated literature or validation status presented
Scope limited to protocol framing; no full SAP or grant narrative generated

inputs.label: Gut microbiome and IBD retrospective omics study

assertions.text: Confirmatory aims clearly distinguished from exploratory analyses
Evidence-type alignment is stated for each aim
Scope and failure-mode audit section present
No unverified citations presented as established fact
Output includes a recommended final aim package with brief rationale

inputs.label: Broad PD-L1/immune evasion idea converted to testable TNBC aims

assertions.text: Broad topic successfully narrowed to a specific primary aim before hypothesis design
Speculative ambitions labeled as exploratory rather than confirmatory
Aim scope control applied — no aim sprawl
No clinical recommendations made
Self-critical review section present identifying weakest assumption

inputs.label: Dual causal and predictive aims in real-world anticoagulation safety study

assertions.text: Causal aim and predictive aim are treated separately with distinct hypothesis logic
Required evidence type specified for each aim
No conflation of association with causation in wording
Feasibility assumptions explicitly labeled
Output does not fabricate drug safety data or real-world effect sizes

key_strengths: Exceptionally strong aim-hierarchy discipline with 15 hard rules enforcing testability and scope control
Seven reference modules cover all major failure modes: scope inflation, circular hypotheses, confirmatory overclaim
Mandatory 8-section output structure (A–J) ensures completeness and reviewer-defensible framing
Strong scientific integrity: explicit prohibition on fabricating references, feasibility claims, and validation status

recommendations.title: Add structured clarification prompt for under-specified inputs
recommendations.problem: When input is vague, the skill proceeds with labeled assumptions but does not actively ask for the 2–4 minimum clarifying inputs that would most improve accuracy.
recommendations.root_cause: The Input Validation section lists valid input types but does not define a minimum clarification protocol when critical items are missing.
recommendations.fix: Add a Clarification Rule block: if disease/condition or intended evidence type is absent, ask exactly 2–4 high-yield follow-up questions before generating the full output.

recommendations.title: Description trigger language lacks user-natural phrasing
recommendations.problem: The description is technically accurate but uses protocol-design vocabulary that non-expert users may not naturally employ when asking for help.
recommendations.root_cause: Description written for expert researchers; casual or clinical users may not recognize 'aim hierarchy' or 'protocol-framing structure' as terms to search with.
recommendations.fix: Expand description to include natural user phrases like 'turn my research idea into specific aims', 'help me write research questions and hypotheses', or 'design study objectives'.

recommendations.title: SKILL.md approaching 500-line threshold
recommendations.problem: At 350 lines, the SKILL.md is within bounds but some execution logic could be offloaded to references to reduce load time.
recommendations.root_cause: Core execution steps (Step 1–8) and Hard Rules occupy significant space that could be partially extracted.
recommendations.fix: Consider moving the 15 Hard Rules to references/hard-rules.md and keeping only a summary in SKILL.md to create headroom for future additions.
